title: Scream
logline: A high school student becomes the target of a masked killer one year after her mother's murder, forcing her to confront both her traumatic past and a deadly game where knowing horror movie rules might be the only way to survive.
antagonistOrConflict: Two masked killers (Billy Loomis and Stu Macher) seeking revenge and thrills
tone: Self-aware, suspenseful, darkly comedic, meta-textual about horror conventions
themes: The blurred line between fiction and reality
The consequences of violence as entertainment
Female agency and final girl tropes

actBreaks.description: Sidney is attacked in her own home and Billy is arrested with the cell phone, establishing him as the prime suspect and forcing Sidney into protective custody, which shifts the story from setup to investigation.

validation.ruling: Scene #32 (p45, 37.5%) marks Sidney's first public confrontation with reporter Gale after the attack—a point where private terror becomes public spectacle, fundamentally shifting the story's trajectory and raising stakes as media circus intensifies.

actBreaks.description: The party at Stu's house begins in earnest while Gale stakes out the location, converging all characters at the isolated house where the climactic confrontation will unfold.

validation.ruling: Scene #79 (p96, 80%) marks entry into the final house siege sequence. Though content unavailable, position and context indicate the point of no return into the climactic confrontation—the story's final irreversible shift into resolution mode.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script follows a classic three-act slasher structure but subverts it through constant meta-commentary. Act One (scenes 1-32, pages 1-45) establishes the rules through Casey's brutal opener and Sidney's first attack, creating paranoia around everyone. Act Two (scenes 33-79, pages 46-79) is an extended investigation and escalation phase where red herrings multiply, the killer taunts Sidney repeatedly, and the media circus intensifies—this act is notably long, building dread through repetition and false scares. Act Three (scenes 80-98, pages 79-120) is the extended finale at Stu's party, a 40-page sustained climax that systematically eliminates characters and reveals the dual-killer twist. The structure is intentionally formulaic to comment on formula itself.

What's distinctive is how the script uses scene count (98 scenes in 120 pages) to create relentless pacing—short scenes that cut rapidly between perspectives, phone calls that span multiple scenes, and cross-cutting that maintains multiple tension threads. The structure front-loads violence (the Casey sequence is 19 pages), then shifts to psychological warfare in the middle, then explodes into chaos for the final act.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing operates in waves. The opening Casey sequence (scenes 1-11) is a slow-burn 19 pages that establishes maximum dread. Then the script accelerates dramatically—scenes 15-32 cover Sidney's school day and first attack in just 13 pages. The middle section (scenes 33-67) deliberately slows to let paranoia build through interrogations and conversations, with brief punctuation marks of violence. Scene 67 onward races toward the party, and once scene 79 hits, the script never relents—the final 40 pages are essentially one continuous sequence of reveals, chases, and confrontations with rapid scene cuts (scenes 92-98 are all marked 'continuous'). The rhythm mimics a heartbeat: long dread, sharp spike, sustained terror.

incitingIncident.description: Casey receives multiple phone calls from an unknown man who wants to talk, asks about her popcorn and video, and engages her in flirtatious but increasingly unsettling conversation.

validation.ruling: Scene #1 has no content available for evaluation. Scene #3 (p3, INT. KITCHEN, 12pp) is the opening attack sequence that shatters the status quo, establishes the killer threat, and launches the central conflict—meeting all core inciting incident criteria at the ideal 2.5% position.

midpoint.description: At Blockbuster, Randy tells Stu the mass murder section had a run in sales, and Randy suggests Billy would make a good suspect planning his next kill while police investigate dead ends.

validation.ruling: Scene #49 (p67, Principal's office party planning) is a transitional scene but lacks the transformative power of a true midpoint. Scene #53 (p71, INT. BLOCKBUSTER, 2pp at 59.2%) better positions as the midpoint where Randy discusses horror movie rules—a meta moment that shifts Sidney from reactive victim to aware participant who understands the game's stakes, raising tension before the third act house party.

climax.description: Billy rises from the dead revealing he and Stu are the killers working together, they explain their plan to frame Sidney's father, Billy admits he killed Sidney's mother for sleeping with his father, and they stab each other to create their alibi before hunting Sidney and attacking Randy.

validation.ruling: Scene #97 (p106, 12pp at 88.3%) is the definitive climax: Sidney confronts both suspects with a gun, must choose who to trust, highest dramatic tension, killer reveal and final confrontation all occur here. Meets all climactic criteria perfectly.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASEY'S HOUSE - NIGB'l' - ESTABLISHING
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN
scenes.summary: Casey receives multiple phone calls from an unknown man who wants to talk, asks about her popcorn and video, and engages her in flirtatious but increasingly unsettling conversation.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE OF BOUSE
scenes.summary: The caller threatens Casey not to hang up or she'll die like her mother, a lawn chair crashes through the kitchen glass door, and a masked figure rushes at her as she grabs a knife and flees.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct attack by the killer, escalating from phone harassment to physical violence

scenes.heading: EXT. CORNER OF HOUSE
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: EXT. DRIVEWAY
scenes.summary: Casey moves along the house to a window and sees the masked figure staring back at her; he crashes through the glass, grabs her neck, but she breaks free as his hands disappear inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT DOOR
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER
scenes.summary: Casey's parents arrive home to find the front door ajar and the shattered patio door; the father searches frantically while Casey collapses outside being dragged through the soil by the killer, still clutching the phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT DOOR
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - SAME NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: EXT. ~
scenes.summary: The father tells his wife to drive to the Mackenzies; she rushes out and screams, leading both parents to discover their daughter's body hanging from a tree in the front yard.
scenes.turningPointNote: Casey's murder is revealed, establishing the brutal stakes and launching the main investigation

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Sidney screams and pulls away from a masked figure in her bedroom, which turns out to be her boyfriend Billy Loomis who climbed through her window to surprise her despite her father being home.

scenes.heading: INT. SIDNEY'S BEDROOM - EARLY MORNING
scenes.summary: Sidney's father tries to enter her room but the door jams; Billy hides behind the bed while Sidney talks to her father about homework, then Billy prepares to leave through the window after discussing their relationship and sex.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALR
scenes.summary: Sidney watches her father drive away, then approaches Bayboro High School where six police cars, news vans, reporters including Gale Weathers, and crowds have gathered following Casey's murder.

scenes.heading: INT. PRINCIPAL'S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Mrs. Tate addresses the class about the tragedy and need for prayer; Sidney stares at Casey's empty desk when she's called to the principal's office where Sheriff Burke and others wait.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL COURTYARD- LATER
scenes.summary: Sidney is questioned by Sheriff Burke and Dewey; at lunch with her friends, they discuss the police asking if they like to hunt because the victims were gutted, which upsets Sidney.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDNEY'S HOUSE- LATER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Sidney calls Tatum to confirm she can stay over since her dad won't be back until Sunday, and they discuss the police and reporters bringing back memories of her mother's murder.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Sidney packs an overnight bag and turns on the TV, switching channels through news reports about the murders until she sees Gale Weathers reporting on her mother Maureen Prescott's rape and murder a year ago, then turns it off.

scenes.heading: INT. UPSTAIRS LANDING
scenes.summary: Sidney answers the phone thinking it's Randy but the caller says if she hangs up she'll die like her mother; enraged, Sidney hangs up and is immediately attacked by a masked figure bursting from the hall closet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sidney becomes the killer's target, shifting the story's focus from Casey's murder to Sidney's survival

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Sidney locks herself in her bedroom using the closet door to jam the entrance as the figure pounds against it; she finds the phone is dead and escapes through the window.

scenes.heading: INT. LANDING
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Billy climbs through Sidney's window claiming he heard screaming; a cellular phone falls from his jeans and Sidney sees it, their eyes meet, then she flees as sirens approach and Billy reaches for her; Sidney opens the front door and screams seeing a ghost mask, which Dewey is holding.
scenes.turningPointNote: Billy becomes the prime suspect when his cell phone is discovered, creating doubt about his innocence

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET
scenes.summary: Gale Weathers arrives in her news van as Sidney is escorted to a squad car; Gale spots Sidney and tries to interview Tatum, who tells her to go fuck herself before driving away.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION- LATER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. SHERIFF'S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Sidney sits in the sheriff's office wearing his jacket while Dewey tries to locate her father at the Hilton but can't find him registered; Billy is questioned with his father present about the cellular phone found at Sidney's house.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE BULL PEN - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Tatum comforts Sidney at the station while Burke and Dewey discuss that the ghost mask is sold at Kroger's and WalMart without purchase records and they're waiting on Billy's phone bill; Tatum asks Dewey to take them home.

scenes.heading: EXT. POLICE STATION- SIDE DOOR
scenes.summary: Sheriff Burke tells the group to use the back exit to avoid reporters, but Gale Weathers and Kenny ambush Sidney in the alley with a camera; Sidney confronts Gale about her book blaming Cotton Weary.

scenes.heading: INT. TATUM'S BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Gale tells Sidney her book will be out later this year and offers to send a copy; Sidney punches Gale in the face, knocking her and Kenny to the pavement; later at Tatum's house, they laugh about it while Dewey brings ice for Sidney's hand.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Sidney answers the phone and hears the killer's voice again; he taunts her about Billy being innocent and says this will be fun like old times before hanging up, leaving Sidney frozen while Dewey rushes out in his boxers with his gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. BAYBORO MAIN STREET- DAWN - ESTABLISHING
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Sidney and Tatum eat breakfast while watching TV news about Cotton Weary awaiting appeal for Sidney's mother's murder; Dewey tells Sidney that Billy was released because his phone bill was clean and he didn't make the calls.
scenes.turningPointNote: Billy is cleared of suspicion, eliminating the obvious suspect and deepening the mystery of the killer's identity

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL STREET - LATER
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. PATROL JEEP
scenes.summary: Dewey drives Sidney to school, assuring her she'll be safe there; reporters swarm Sidney with microphones asking how it feels to almost be brutally butchered.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEWSVAN - STREET
scenes.summary: Sidney tells Tatum she needs to talk to someone and approaches Gale's newsvan; Gale initially refuses to talk but Sidney requests an off-the-record conversation with no camera, and Gale agrees after sending Kenny away, discussing her theory that Cotton Weary is innocent.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL CAMPUS - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Tatum and Sidney walk across campus when a figure in a ghost mask falls in step behind them; Tatum reassures Sidney about being safe at school while discussing serial killers.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Two students in ghost masks close in on Sidney making her scream, but they laugh and run off as pranks; a TV reporter notes several students wearing the killer's mask type at school.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Sidney's friends discuss Billy's innocence when a masked student runs screaming down the hall; Sidney gets upset and leaves, with Billy chasing after her while Tatum yells at him to stay away.

scenes.heading: INT. GIRL'S BATHROOM
scenes.summary: Billy catches Sidney in the empty hallway and they argue about her need for time since her mother's death anniversary is tomorrow; Sidney angrily tells Billy some people aren't as perfect at coping and enters the girls' bathroom, leaving Billy alone and frustrated.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. PRINCIPAL'S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney checks bathroom stalls for the killer when two feet step down in the last stall; she slips on the wet floor, glimpses a ghost mask in the mirror, and crashes through the exit door into the hallway where she collides with Principal Himbry who's confronting two students in ghost masks.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL STREET - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Principal Himbry angrily berates two students in ghost masks about fairness when Sidney bursts in hysterical saying the killer is there; Himbry comforts her while Dewey investigates at the school, encountering Gale who flirts with him.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL CORRIDOR - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Tatum escorts Sidney down the emptying school hallway, trying to convince her it was just a sick prank, though Sidney insists it was really the killer.

scenes.heading: INT. PRINCIPAL'S OFFICE - I.ATER
scenes.summary: Stu invites Sidney and Tatum to a party at his house since his parents are out of town, promising safety in numbers; Sidney reluctantly agrees to come.
scenes.turningPointNote: The party invitation sets up the climactic location where the final confrontation will occur

scenes.heading: INT. OUTER OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scene content not provided in CONTENT section.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mr. Himbry investigates mysterious knocking at his office door, finds no one in the empty corridor, and returns to discover his closet door now closed when he had left it open.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET - LATER
scenes.summary: Tatum and Sidney arrive at a house while a ghost-masked figure watches unnoticed from across the street under a tree, and Billy catches up with Stu on main street.

scenes.heading: INT. BLOCKBUSTER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At Blockbuster, Randy tells Stu the mass murder section had a run in sales, and Randy suggests Billy would make a good suspect planning his next kill while police investigate dead ends.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET - LITTLE LATER
scenes.summary: Characters travel on Main Street.

scenes.heading: INT. PATROL JEEP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Billy confronts Randy about accusing him, and Randy jokingly admits he'd be the first to die in a horror movie.

scenes.heading: EXT. PATROL JEEP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney observes the deserted main street storefronts closed for curfew and Tatum jokes about a movie being made about her, while Dewey parks at the police station.

scenes.heading: EXT. POLICE STATION - SHERIFF'S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Sheriff Burke urgently questions Dewey about his whereabouts and confirms Sidney's father is still missing, ordering Dewey to stay close to Sidney at Stu's party tonight.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPERMMKET - FEW MINU"l'ES LATER
scenes.summary: Sidney confesses to Tatum that she can't relax when Billy touches her, and Tatum advises her to find something sexy to think about during sex like she does with Grant Goodeve.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUPERMARKET
scenes.summary: Tatum kicks a shopping cart away that rolls down the road and crashes into the ghost-masked figure who stops it cold with one hand.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - NIG!rl'
scenes.summary: Dewey's jeep drives down a winding road through thick woods followed by a white newsvan.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Characters gather in a living room at Stu's house.

scenes.heading: EXT. S'l'U*S BOUSE- ROAD
scenes.summary: Dewey arrives at Stu's isolated house at the end of a dead-end road with the newsvan following behind.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWSVAN
scenes.summary: Gale instructs Kenny to prep a compact camera to hide in a window and record the party, accepting a thirty-second delay on the control board.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN
scenes.summary: Dewey allows Gale to join him in checking out the party, and she secretly takes the camera from Kenny with a wink.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM- FEW MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Randy takes a vote on which horror movie to watch while Stu answers the door to reveal Gale has arrived with Dewey, causing Tatum to react negatively.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN
scenes.summary: Sidney asks Dewey if her father has been found and he says not yet, while Tatum goes to the garage for beer.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE
scenes.summary: Tatum enters the dark garage for beer from the refrigerator, not noticing the kitchen door closing behind her and sealing her in, then the ghost-masked figure attacks her and she gets trapped in the pet door as the garage door rises, killing her.
scenes.turningPointNote: First kill at the party - Tatum's death escalates the immediate danger

scenes.heading: EXT. GARAGE DOOR
scenes.summary: Tatum's body hangs dead from the rising garage door outside.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Gale asks Kenny if a tabloid journalist has ever won a Pulitzer while he monitors the equipment.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Sidney and Billy reconcile in a bedroom, with Sidney admitting she's been self-absorbed and Billy apologizing for being selfish, and Sidney confesses she's scared of becoming like her mother if she has sex.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Teens watch a horror movie while Randy explains the three rules to survive: never have sex, never drink or do drugs, and never say 'I'll be right back,' which Stu immediately says.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWSVAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gale and Kenny watch the party on the monitor from the newsvan while Randy continues explaining horror movie survival rules.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A patrol car drives down a road.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The teens continue watching the horror movie in the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dewey invites Gale to see the town, agreeing too quickly when she asks if Kenny can come along.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - SECONDS.LATER
scenes.summary: Billy blocks the hidden camera's view as he undresses while Sidney's bra straps slide off her shoulders.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWSVAN - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Randy answers the phone and announces that Principal Himbry has been found dead, gutted and hung from the goalpost at the football field.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of Himbry's murder sends partygoers away, isolating remaining characters

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM - MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: After sex, Sidney questions Billy about who he called when arrested, realizing Sheriff Burke called his father, not Billy, and Billy admits he called her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Billy's lie about the phone call reveals his guilt, marking the betrayal revelation

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney flees down the hallway while Billy chases after her.

scenes.heading: INT. DARK SPACE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The ghost-masked figure stabs Billy repeatedly in the chest as Sidney screams, covering her face in blood, and Billy's body falls lifeless to the floor.
scenes.turningPointNote: Billy appears to be killed by the ghost figure, misdirecting the audience

scenes.heading: EXT. DA.RX ROAD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney tries to unlock a door but finds it locked from the other side, forcing her toward the staircase, while Gale and Dewey walk a dark road with a flashlight.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CON'rINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy continues explaining horror movie rules to the remaining teens in the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWSVAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gale and Dewey fall into a ditch and discover Sidney's father's hidden car in the brush.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery of father's car frames him as the killer, adding false lead

scenes.heading: INT. ATTIC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kenny watches the monitor and sees the ghost figure standing behind Randy with a knife raised, screams at the monitor, and bolts out to help.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD
scenes.summary: Sidney climbs into the attic through a window.

scenes.heading: INT. ATTIC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney searches the dark attic.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRON'l' DOOR- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gale and Dewey rush up the driveway to get backup and the camera, but Gale finds Kenny gone from the van and discovers Dewey missing from the patrol jeep after the horn goes off.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney finds her bound and gagged father in the attic.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gale races across the yard away from the house.

scenes.heading: INT. ATTIC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney uses a tennis racket to break the attic window and climbs through broken glass.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE OF BOUSE
scenes.summary: Sidney lowers herself from the side of the house and drops to the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWSVAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gale starts the newsvan's engine and discovers blood smearing the windshield as Randy's hand reaches through the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gale drives erratically, throwing Randy forward and sending Kenny's corpse sliding down the windshield before spinning out and crashing into a tree.

scenes.heading: INT. JEEP
scenes.summary: Sidney locks herself in the patrol jeep and uses the police radio to call for help while the ghost figure crawls into the back unnoticed.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRON'!' OF '1EEP
scenes.summary: The ghost grabs Sidney's neck from behind, she fights back and kicks wildly, escapes through the door and runs toward the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy and Stu both beg Sidney to let them in from outside, each claiming the other is the killer, and Sidney aims the gun at both before slamming and locking the door on them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sidney must choose who to trust between Randy and Stu at the climax

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM/FOYER
scenes.summary: Billy rises from the dead revealing he and Stu are the killers working together, they explain their plan to frame Sidney's father, Billy admits he killed Sidney's mother for sleeping with his father, and they stab each other to create their alibi before hunting Sidney and attacking Randy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Major revelation - Billy and Stu are both killers, Billy killed Sidney's mother, full plan exposed

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sidney shoots Billy in the head killing him, fights off Stu with the television and VCR which electrocutes him to death, Randy survives, and Gale's camera captures everything as police sirens approach.
scenes.turningPointNote: Final confrontation - Sidney kills both killers and survives
